images.alt:
Percy's body was a knife, all sharp corners and lean, sinewy lines. Jason was more bullish, stockier and heavy with muscle. Percy looked at him with cool interest, like he could not be bothered to find anything attractive in what he saw. But his green eyes gleamed a ferocious hunger. And his hands reached at once for Jason's body, touching his waist, his stomach, rubbing along the thicker muscles of his arms, his broad shoulders, his wider chest. Jason shivered with every brush of those strong, tanned fingers along the smooth, pale expanse of his skin. It wasn't enough. He needed—more—closer—deeper—
“Percy,” he breathed, his voice hitching in the back of his throat.
Percy hummed. “That's it,” he whispered into Jason's ear. He leaned forward into Jason's space to say it. He was a little shorter than Jason. He had to stand on his tiptoes. He had to reach for it. Jason almost whimpered. The son of the sea god, the champion against the Titan Kronos, the most powerful demigod Jason had ever encountered—he had to strain a little just to put his mouth to Jason's ear. “That's it, Jay,” Percy whispered, and Jason could hear the smile in his voice, “come apart for me.”

images.alt: This infographic illustrates the two sides of high-functioning AuDHD. The left side depicts the traits that people typically see in individuals with this condition, such as creativity, proactiveness, confidence, quick-thinking, organization, high achievement, proactiveness, social skills, hard work, a high energy level, meeting deadlines, appearing calm, and enthusiasm. The right side highlights the internal struggles that are often hidden, including excessive worrying, rejection sensitivity, perfectionism, sensory sensitivity, frequent exhaustion, hyperfocus, fear of failure, procrastination, inattention, restlessness, daydreaming, and self-doubt.  This visual representation emphasizes the importance of understanding both the visible and invisible aspects of high-functioning AuDHD.
